bibliographic_data.title: Joint Multivariate and Functional Modeling for Plant Traits and Reflectances
bibliographic_data.abstract: The investigation of leaf-level traits in response to varying environmental conditions has immense importance for understanding plant ecology. Remote sensing technology enables measurement of the reflectance of plants to make inferences about underlying traits along environmental gradients. While much focus has been placed on understanding how reflectance and traits are related at the leaf-level, the challenge of modeling the dependence of this relationship along environmental gradients has limited this line of inquiry. Here, we take up the problem of jointly modeling traits and reflectance given environment. Our objective is to assess not only response to environmental regressors but also dependence between trait levels and the reflectance spectrum in the context of this regression. This leads to joint modeling of a response vector of traits with reflectance arising as a functional response over the wavelength spectrum.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a novel approach for jointly modeling plant traits and reflectances given environmental conditions, addressing the challenge of modeling their dependence along environmental gradients.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors employ joint multivariate and function-on-scalar regression to model both trait vectors and functional reflectance spectra in response to environmental features.
outcomes: Developed a method for jointly modeling traits and reflectances
Assessed the relationship between plant traits and their reflectance spectrum under varying environmental conditions
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study of leaf-level traits is crucial for understanding plant ecology, particularly how these traits vary along spatial-environmental gradients. Remote sensing technology allows for efficient measurement of plant reflectance to infer underlying traits. However, the challenge lies in modeling the dependence between reflectance and traits across environmental conditions. The authors propose a joint multivariate and function-on-scalar regression model to address this issue. By using data from the Greater Cape Floristic Region in South Africa, they aim to assess both the response of plant traits to environmental factors and their relationship with the reflectance spectrum. This approach provides insights into how ecosystems may change under novel environments.
